title: quercus longispica
intro: Quercus longispica, also known as the long-bracted oak, is a species of evergreen tree that belongs to the beech family (Fagaceae). The medicinal properties of Quercus longispica include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant compounds, which contribute to its potential health benefits. The health benefits of Quercus longispica include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting immune function. Herbal preparations of Quercus longispica can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are traditionally used to treat various ailments, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
